title: Input Validation — Decisions
description: Add input validation to your inventory program using if/else statements. Validate that price is greater than 0 and quantity is >= 0, printing error messages for invalid input. Add a switch statement to handle the menu choice (1-6), with a default case for invalid choices. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
test_code:
assert 'if' in cpp, 'Use if statements for input validation'
assert 'else' in cpp, 'Use else to handle invalid input cases'
assert 'switch' in cpp, 'Use a switch statement to handle menu choices'
assert 'case' in cpp, 'Switch statement needs case labels for each menu option'
assert 'default' in cpp, 'Include a default case for invalid menu choices'
assert 'price' in cpp.lower(), 'Validate the price value'
assert 'quantity' in cpp.lower() or 'qty' in cpp.lower(), 'Validate the quantity value'
assert '> 0' in cpp or '>0' in cpp or '<= 0' in cpp or '<=0' in cpp or '< 0' in cpp or '<0' in cpp, 'Check that price is positive (> 0 or handle <= 0)'
acceptance_criteria: Uses switch statement to handle menu choices 1-6
Includes a default case for invalid menu choices
Prints appropriate error messages for invalid input
hints: A switch statement is ideal for menu selection: switch(choice) { case 1: ... break; }
Don't forget the break statement after each case
Use if (price <= 0) to catch zero and negative prices
The default case handles any choice not matching 1-6
